---
title: "Data Exploration"
subtitle: "Cohort, sessions, and environmental records"
author: "ASD Digital Twin Research"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  warning: false
jupyter: python3
---

## Overview

This notebook is the entry point. It loads the three CSVs produced by `data/data_generation.py` and provides a guided tour of what's there. Everything downstream depends on these structures.

```{python}
import warnings; warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

cohort = pd.read_csv("../data/synthetic_cohort.csv")
sessions = pd.read_csv("../data/sessions.csv")
env = pd.read_csv("../data/environmental.csv")

print(f"Cohort:        {len(cohort):>7,} children")
print(f"Sessions:      {len(sessions):>7,} session records")
print(f"Environmental: {len(env):>7,} daily records")
```

## Cohort summary

```{python}
print("Severity distribution:")
print(cohort["severity"].value_counts(normalize=True).round(3))
print("\nSex distribution:")
print(cohort["sex"].value_counts(normalize=True).round(3))
print(f"\nAge: mean={cohort.age.mean():.1f}, range=[{cohort.age.min()}, {cohort.age.max()}]")
print(f"\nADOS-2 CALSS: mean={cohort.ados_calss.mean():.1f}")
print(f"SRS-2 T-score: mean={cohort.srs2_t.mean():.1f}")
print(f"\nPollen-sensitive: {cohort.pollen_sensitive.mean():.1%}")
print(f"Pressure-sensitive: {cohort.pressure_sensitive.mean():.1%}")
```

## Session-level distributions

```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(13, 7))

# Sessions per child
sessions_per_child = sessions.groupby("child_id").size()
axes[0,0].hist(sessions_per_child, bins=30, color="#7a9b6e", edgecolor="#1a2332")
axes[0,0].set_title("Sessions per child")
axes[0,0].set_xlabel("n sessions"); axes[0,0].set_ylabel("n children")

# Session type mix
sessions["session_type"].value_counts().plot(
    kind="barh", color="#3a4f7a", ax=axes[0,1])
axes[0,1].set_title("Session type distribution")

# Outcomes
outcomes = pd.Series({
    "Success":   sessions["session_success"].mean(),
    "Incident":  sessions["behavioral_incident"].mean(),
    "Substitute": sessions["is_substitute"].mean(),
    "Family stress": sessions["family_stress_event"].mean(),
    "Low sleep":   sessions["low_sleep_night"].mean(),
})
axes[0,2].bar(range(len(outcomes)), outcomes.values * 100,
              color=["#7a9b6e", "#c47b85", "#c97a4a", "#d4a04a", "#3a4f7a"],
              edgecolor="#1a2332")
axes[0,2].set_xticks(range(len(outcomes)))
axes[0,2].set_xticklabels(outcomes.index, rotation=30, ha="right")
axes[0,2].set_ylabel("% of sessions")
axes[0,2].set_title("Outcome and risk-factor base rates")

# Treatment integrity distribution
axes[1,0].hist(sessions["treatment_integrity_pct"], bins=30,
               color="#c97a4a", edgecolor="#1a2332")
axes[1,0].axvline(80, color="red", ls="--", label="ABA threshold (80%)")
axes[1,0].set_xlabel("Treatment integrity %"); axes[1,0].set_ylabel("n sessions")
axes[1,0].set_title("Treatment integrity")
axes[1,0].legend()

# Time of day
sessions["time_of_day"].value_counts().plot(
    kind="bar", color="#d4a04a", ax=axes[1,1], edgecolor="#1a2332")
axes[1,1].set_title("Time of day")
axes[1,1].tick_params(axis='x', rotation=0)

# Day of week
dow_labels = ["Mon", "Tue", "Wed", "Thu", "Fri", "Sat", "Sun"]
sessions["day_of_week"].value_counts().sort_index().plot(
    kind="bar", color="#9b6cc4", ax=axes[1,2], edgecolor="#1a2332")
axes[1,2].set_xticklabels([dow_labels[i] for i in range(7) if i in sessions.day_of_week.unique()],
                           rotation=0)
axes[1,2].set_title("Sessions by day of week")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("../reports/figures/eda_session_distributions.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()
```

## Environmental data — sanity checks

```{python}
# Pressure swings should be relatively rare
print(f"Days with |Δpressure| > 5 hPa: {env['pressure_swing'].mean():.1%}")
print(f"High pollen days: {env['high_pollen'].mean():.1%}")
print(f"School days: {env['is_school_day'].mean():.1%}")
print(f"Break-transition days: {env['break_transition'].mean():.1%}")

# Plot temperature seasonality for one zip
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 4))
for zip_code in env["zip"].unique():
    sub = env[env["zip"] == zip_code].copy()
    sub["date"] = pd.to_datetime(sub["date"])
    sub = sub.sort_values("date")
    ax.plot(sub["date"], sub["temp_c"], label=zip_code, alpha=0.7, lw=1)
ax.set_xlabel("Date"); ax.set_ylabel("Temperature (°C)")
ax.set_title("Daily temperature by zip code — sanity check on seasonality")
ax.legend(loc="upper right", frameon=False)
ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("../reports/figures/eda_temperature.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()
```

## Outcome rates by demographic strata

```{python}
strata = sessions.merge(cohort[["child_id", "severity", "age", "sex"]], on="child_id")

print("Success rate by severity:")
print(strata.groupby("severity")["session_success"].agg(["mean", "count"]).round(3))

print("\nSuccess rate by age band:")
strata["age_band"] = pd.cut(strata["age"], bins=[2, 6, 9, 12, 17],
                              labels=["3-6", "7-9", "10-12", "13-17"])
print(strata.groupby("age_band", observed=True)["session_success"].agg(["mean", "count"]).round(3))

print("\nSuccess rate by sex:")
print(strata.groupby("sex")["session_success"].agg(["mean", "count"]).round(3))
```

The base-rate gradient by severity is consistent with published ABA outcome literature — children with more substantial support needs achieve criterion-level mastery on a smaller fraction of sessions, on average. This is descriptive, not normative.

## Next steps

- `02_baseline_models.qmd` — fit Tier 1 and Tier 2 models, establish what session-only data can predict
- `03_butterfly_effects.qmd` — the headline analysis, adds the environmental layer
- `04_twin_validation.qmd` — Bayesian hierarchical model, out-of-time validation
